title: The Peach Orchard
introKo:
Rose 부인의 친구, Peach 부인네 복숭아 과수원에서 수확을 돕는 이야기.
아주 건전해요, 표면적으로는요. 단어를 탭하면 속뜻이 보여요.

quiz.q: 가장 좋은 복숭아는 어디에 열린다고 했나요?

normal.text: Summer comes to the village. Mrs. Rose has a friend, Mrs. Peach, who owns a peach orchard on the hill.
"Her trees are heavy with fruit," Mrs. Rose says. "She needs help with the harvest. Will you come with me?"
Mrs. Peach waits at the gate. "Welcome," she smiles. "My peaches are round and ripe. They are waiting for the right hands."

options.en: I can't wait to get started.
options.reaction: "I like that," Mrs. Peach says. "An eager helper is the best kind."

options.en: How do I know which ones are ready?
options.reaction: "Oh, you will know," she says. "A ripe one tells you. Just listen with your fingers."

normal.text: She shows you the first tree. "Hold a peach softly," she says. "Squeeze it just a little. If it gives, it is ready for you."
You touch one. The skin is warm from the sun, and covered with soft fuzz. "Gentle," she says. "A ripe peach loves a slow hand."

options.en: I think I'm a natural at this.
options.ko: 저 이거 소질이 있는 것 같아요.
options.reaction: "Confident, too," she laughs. "Then the sweetest fruit will find you first."
"Then it is ready," she smiles. "The soft ones never last long here."

normal.text: You taste one peach. Juice runs down your chin. "Careful," Mrs. Peach laughs. "The good ones are always a little messy."
Then she points up. "The best ones grow on the highest branch. Most people never reach them. Can you climb that high?"
You climb the ladder to the top. The peaches there are the biggest and roundest of all. You fill your basket until it overflows.

options.en: Your orchard is amazing, Mrs. Peach.
options.reaction: "Thank you, dear. Most men give up before the top branch. You did not."

options.en: I could do this all day.
options.reaction: "Careful, dear," she laughs. "Say that in my orchard, and I will hold you to it."

normal.text: You walk home with Mrs. Rose, carrying a full basket. "Well?" she asks. "Now you know why I love her orchard."
That night, your hands still smell of peaches. The sweetest things, you think, are worth getting a little messy for.

hard.text: Summer settles over the village, and Mrs. Rose arrives with news: her old friend Mrs. Peach, who owns the peach orchard on the hill, needs helpers for the harvest.
"Her trees are so heavy with fruit that the branches nearly touch the ground," Mrs. Rose says. "I promised her my hardest worker — that would be you."
Mrs. Peach greets you at the gate with a knowing smile. "Welcome, dear. My peaches are perfectly round and perfectly ripe — they have been waiting all season for the right pair of hands."

options.en: I can't wait to get started.
options.reaction: "I like that," Mrs. Peach says approvingly. "An eager helper is the rarest fruit of all."

options.en: How do I know which ones are ready?
options.reaction: "Oh, you will know," she says. "A ripe one tells you itself. You simply have to listen with your fingers."

hard.text: She leads you to the first tree. "Hold a peach softly and squeeze it just a little," she instructs. "If it gives under your fingers, it is ready for you."
You touch one. The skin, warm from the afternoon sun and covered with the softest fuzz, seems to rest in your hand. "Gently now," she says. "A ripe peach loves a slow, patient hand."

options.en: I think I'm a natural at this.
options.ko: 저 이거 소질이 있는 것 같아요.
options.reaction: "Confident hands," she laughs softly. "Then the sweetest fruit will find you before long."
"Then it is more than ready," she smiles. "The soft ones never stay on the tree for long."

hard.text: You taste one peach, and juice runs down your chin before you can stop it. "Careful," Mrs. Peach laughs. "The really good ones are always a little messy."
Then she points toward the top of the tallest tree. "The finest ones grow on the highest branch, where most people never bother to reach. Do you think you can climb that high?"
You climb the ladder until you reach the very top, where the biggest and roundest peaches hang. You fill your basket until it overflows.

options.en: Your orchard is truly amazing, Mrs. Peach.
options.reaction: "Thank you, dear. Most men give up long before the top branch. You, however, did not."

options.en: I could do this all day.
options.reaction: "Careful, dear," she laughs. "Say such things in my orchard, and I will certainly hold you to it."

hard.text: You walk home beside Mrs. Rose, carrying a basket that threatens to overflow. "Well?" she asks knowingly. "Now you understand why I love her orchard so much."
That night, the smell of peaches lingers on your hands. The sweetest things, you decide, are always worth getting a little messy for.

0.name: 신중한 감별사
0.en: The next week, you come back with Mrs. Rose and sort peaches at the table by the gate. You never go past the first row of trees, but your baskets are the neatest in the orchard. "The trees trust you," Mrs. Peach says softly, "and so do I."
0.ko: 다음 주에도 Rose 부인과 함께 과수원을 찾아, 대문 옆 탁자에서 복숭아를 골라 담아요. 첫 줄 나무 너머로는 가 본 적 없지만, 당신의 바구니는 과수원에서 제일 단정해요. "나무들이 당신을 믿어요. 나도 그렇고요," Peach 부인이 나직이 말해요.

1.name: 느긋한 수확꾼
1.en: You visit the orchard again that weekend, taking your time under the trees. Mrs. Peach teaches you to find the shy fruit that hides behind the leaves. "A patient hand," she says, "always gets the sweetest reward."
1.ko: 그 주말에 다시 과수원을 찾아 나무 아래서 느긋하게 시간을 보내요. Peach 부인은 잎사귀 뒤에 숨은 수줍은 열매를 찾는 법을 알려줘요. "참을성 있는 손이 언제나 가장 달콤한 보상을 받는 법이에요," 부인이 말해요.

2.name: 넉살 좋은 일손
2.en: A few days later, Mrs. Peach invites you back — this time without Mrs. Rose. "You learn fast," she says, handing you the ladder with a smile. "Today we will see how high you can really go."
2.ko: 며칠 뒤, Peach 부인이 당신을 다시 불러요 — 이번엔 Rose 부인 없이요. "배움이 빠르네요," 부인이 웃으며 사다리를 건네요. "오늘은 당신이 정말 얼마나 높이 올라갈 수 있는지 볼 거예요."

3.name: 가장 높은 가지의 주인
3.en: By the end of summer, you know every tree in the orchard by hand. Mrs. Peach gives you your own key to the gate. "The highest branches are yours now," she says, "and the season is still long."
3.ko: 여름이 끝날 무렵, 당신은 과수원의 나무를 전부 손끝으로 외우고 있어요. Peach 부인은 당신에게 대문 열쇠를 아예 하나 내어줘요. "이제 가장 높은 가지들은 다 당신 거예요," 부인이 말해요. "그리고 이번 철은 아직 길거든요."

summer.en: the hottest season of the year

friend.en: a person you like and know well

peach.en: a soft sweet fruit with fuzzy skin

who.en: the person that; asks about people

orchard.en: a field full of fruit trees

fruit.en: sweet food from trees and plants

gate.en: a door in a fence or wall

welcome.en: a friendly hello to a guest

get.en: to become; get started means begin

started.en: past of start; get started: begin

know.en: to have facts in your mind

ready.en: ripe and good to pick now

fingers.en: the long parts of your hand

skin.en: the outside of fruit or people

sun.en: the bright star in the sky

last.en: to stay good a long time

taste.en: to try food in your mouth

grow.en: to get bigger on a plant

climb.en: to go up with hands and feet

ladder.en: steps you climb to go up

promised.en: said she would surely do it

leads.en: goes first to show the way

instructs.en: tells you how to do something

rest.en: to sit quietly in one place

find.en: to discover; to see at last

bother.en: to take the trouble to try

hang.en: to be held up from above

beside.en: next to; at the side of

lingers.en: stays around for a long time

shade.en: a cool spot out of sunlight

watches.en: looks at something for a while

taking.ko: (시간을) 들이며 (take의 -ing형, take your time: 천천히 하다)

teaches.en: shows you how to do something

key.en: a small tool that opens locks

yours.en: the thing that belongs to you

wink.peach: 영어에서 peach는 '매력적인 사람'을 가리키는 오래된 은어이기도 해요.
wink.peaches: 영어에서 peach는 '매력적인 사람'을 가리키는 오래된 은어이기도 해요.
wink.ripe: '무르익었다'는 말, 꼭 과일에만 쓰는 건 아니죠.
wink.squeeze: 살짝 쥐어보면 안다는 것 — 꼭 과일 고르는 이야기만은 아닌 것처럼 들릴 수 있어요.
wink.fuzz: 복숭아 솜털 이야기지만, 보드라운 살결 묘사로도 읽힐 수 있어요.
wink.soft: 잘 익어 부드러운 것부터 먼저 없어진다 — 임자가 금방 나타난다는 말로도 들려요.
wink.juice: 즙이 턱을 타고 흐른다는 표현은… 다른 장면에도 어울리는 말이죠.
wink.messy: '좋은 건 원래 좀 흘리게 된다'는 말에 어른들은 다르게 웃을 수 있어요.
wink.climb: '제일 좋은 건 제일 높은 가지에 있다'는 말 — 과일 얘기가 아니라 Peach 부인 자기 얘기로 들릴 수도 있어요.
wink.overflows: 바구니가 넘친다는 건, 넘치도록 만족스러웠다는 뜻으로도 읽혀요.
wink.overflow: 바구니가 넘친다는 건, 넘치도록 만족스러웠다는 뜻으로도 읽혀요.

winkEn.peach: In old slang, a 'peach' is also a very attractive person.
winkEn.peaches: In old slang, a 'peach' is also a very attractive person.
winkEn.ripe: 'Ripe and ready' is not only said about fruit — people say it about people, too.
winkEn.squeeze: A gentle squeeze to see if something 'gives' — a phrase that wanders beyond the fruit stand. ('Main squeeze' even means sweetheart.)
winkEn.fuzz: All this warm, soft-fuzzed skin could describe more than a peach.
winkEn.soft: 'The soft ones never last long' — ripe fruit gets picked fast, and so, they say, do charming people.
winkEn.juice: Juice running down your chin paints a picture that fits other pleasures, too.
winkEn.messy: 'The good ones are always a little messy' — grown-ups may hear that as advice about more than fruit.
winkEn.climb: 'The best ones grow on the highest branch' — Mrs. Peach may not be talking about peaches at all.
winkEn.overflows: A basket filled until it overflows — a polite way of saying the visit was more than satisfying.
winkEn.overflow: A basket filled until it overflows — a polite way of saying the visit was more than satisfying.

introEn:
Helping at Mrs. Peach's hillside orchard.
Perfectly wholesome peach picking. ...on the surface.
